data.title: Nvidia In Crosshairs As Senators Question China Export Controls
data.description:
Technical Analysis

Nvidia is trading 3.9% below its 20-day SMA and 5.5% below its 100-day SMA, keeping the near-term trend pointed down even as the longer-term structure is still being tested. Shares are up 44.67% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 41.93, which sits in neutral territory but leans toward weakening momentum rather than a washed-out oversold setup. Meanwhile, MACD is at -2.2690 and below its signal line at -1.3989, a bearish configuration that suggests rallies are still getting sold.

The combination of RSI in the 30–50 range and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $194.00
  • Key Support: $170.50

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 27, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.74 (Up from 96 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $78.72 Billion (Up from $44.06 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 35.8x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $281.04. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Rosenblatt: Buy (Maintains Target to $325.00) (Mar. 23)
  • Cantor Fitzgerald: Overweight (Maintains Target to $300.00) (Mar. 23)
  • Raymond James: Strong Buy (Raises Target to $323.00) (Mar. 19)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because NVDA carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

NVDA Price Action: Nvidia shares were down 0.65% at $174.50 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: AAR Shares Move On $450 Million Contract With Earnings Report Due Today
data.description:
Contract Details And Scope

The company secured two major U.S. Air Force contracts valued at up to approximately $450 million, strengthening its revenue visibility and operational stability.

AAR Corp. won two pallet contracts, including a $160 million firm-fixed-price agreement to provide repair services for the 463L Legacy Cargo Pallet through March 2031, and a $290 million contract to manufacture and produce 463 legacy air cargo pallets through March 2032.

The 463L pallet is the U.S. Air Force's standard cargo platform, and AAR has served as its primary supplier since 1963.

"Government contracts are core to AAR’s diversified business model. For decades, AAR has proudly met the pallet needs of the U.S. government, and these additional awards extend our services into 2032, bringing support and stability wherever they are needed worldwide," said Tom Hoferer.

Earnings Outlook And Recent Developments

AAR is set to report third-quarter earnings after the closing bell today (March 24).

Analysts expect earnings of $1.16 per share, up from 99 cents a year earlier, on revenue of $812.58 million compared to $678.2 million last year.

AIR RSI 41.50; MACD -1.5210

Currently, AAR is trading 6.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 4.0% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating a mixed short-term trend. Shares have increased 47.91% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 41.50, which is considered neutral territory, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD shows a value of -1.5210, with the signal line at -0.4330, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of a neutral RSI and a bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, suggesting traders should watch for potential shifts in market sentiment.

  • Key Resistance: $110.00
  • Key Support: $95.00

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $106.13. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Truist Securities: Buy (Raises Target to $128.00) (Mar. 6)
  • Jefferies: Buy (Raises Target to $135.00) (Feb. 23)
  • Goldman Sachs: Initiated with Neutral (Target $121.00) (Jan. 26)

AIR Momentum Rank 92.2

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for AAR, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 60.79 — The stock is moderately valued compared to peers.
  • Growth Rank: 34.07 — Indicates lower growth potential relative to the market.
  • Quality Rank: 51.54 — Suggests a balanced quality profile.
  • Momentum Rank: 92.2 — Indicates strong momentum, outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: AAR’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-heavy profile with strong momentum, suggesting that the stock may continue to perform well in the near term.

ARWG Holds AIR at 2.66%

  • Archer Growth ETF (NASDAQ:ARWG): 2.66% Weight

Significance: Because AIR carries meaningful weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

AIR Stock Price Activity: AAR shares were up 0.27% at $103.77 at last check on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: QuantumScape Stock Falls Tuesday: What's Behind The Weakness?
data.description:
• QuantumScape stock is showing weakness. Why is QS stock retreating?

Monday’s Oil Sell-Off Benefits QS

President Donald Trump said early Monday that the U.S. would pause strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure for five days after what he described as productive talks with Tehran, sending oil prices sharply lower.

That backdrop may have helped lift QuantumScape on Monday because lower oil prices can improve sentiment across the broader auto and growth-stock complex, even if the direct impact on EV adoption is more nuanced.

Falling energy prices can ease inflation concerns, improve the outlook for consumer spending and support expectations for stronger overall vehicle demand.

For speculative EV-related names like QuantumScape, that kind of macro relief can be enough to drive momentum as traders rotate back into higher-beta technology and auto-adjacent stocks.

Renewed Iran Tensions Weigh On QS Tuesday

That shift likely pressured stocks Tuesday by undermining Monday’s relief trade and pushing investors away from speculative growth names like QuantumScape.

QS RSI Sits Near Lower End Of Neutral Range

QuantumScape's RSI has spent most of the past year in the neutral range, with occasional spikes into overbought territory above 70 and brief dips near oversold levels around 30.

Recently, RSI has been hovering in the lower end of the neutral zone after touching near-oversold conditions, suggesting weakening momentum but not yet an extreme selloff.

QS Shares Edge Lower Tuesday Morning

QS Price Action: QuantumScape shares were down 3.12% at $6.83 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Netgear Stock Soars Tuesday: Here's Why
data.description:
NETGEAR, Inc. (NASDAQ:NTGR) shares are trading higher Tuesday after the FCC added internet routers made in foreign countries to its covered list of prohibited products.

  • Netgear stock is among today’s top performers. Why is NTGR stock surging?

FCC Adds Foreign-Made Routers To Covered List

The FCC updated its Covered List to include all consumer-grade routers produced in foreign countries following a national security determination by Executive Branch agencies. 

The agencies concluded that foreign-produced routers pose "unacceptable risks" to U.S. national security and could introduce supply chain vulnerabilities and cybersecurity threats capable of disrupting critical infrastructure. 

Under the updated rules, newly manufactured foreign-made routers will not be eligible for FCC equipment authorization, effectively preventing new models from being imported or sold in the United States. 

The FCC noted that the restrictions apply only to new device models and do not impact previously approved products already on the market or in use. Officials also cited past cyber incidents involving foreign-made routers, including attacks targeting U.S. infrastructure, as part of the rationale for the decision.

The move could benefit U.S.-based manufacturers like Netgear by limiting competition from foreign-made routers in future product cycles. Netgear offers reliable, easy-to-use, high-performance networking solutions, including switches, routers, access points, software and AV over IP technologies.

Netgear Stock Surges Tuesday

NTGR Price Action: At the time of writing, Netgear shares are trading 15.19% higher at $25.41, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

This illustration was generated using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.
